summary: P0 Bug Fix Verified: Server-side filtering for Leads List page is working correctly. All filters (time_filter, territory, state, assigned_to) are now passed to the backend API instead of client-side filtering.
passed_tests: Backend: territory filter returns filtered leads
Backend: assigned_to filter returns only leads assigned to that user
Backend: state filter returns filtered leads
Backend: pagination works with filters applied
Frontend: time_filter dropdown triggers API call with time_filter parameter
Frontend: territory dropdown triggers API call with territory parameter
Frontend: assigned_to dropdown triggers API call with assigned_to parameter
Frontend: lead count updates correctly based on server response (2 for this_week, 66 for lifetime)
critical_code_review_comments: api.js: leadsAPI.getAll correctly passes time_filter, territory, and assigned_to parameters to the API
LeadsList.js: useEffect dependencies now include all filters (territory, assigned_to, timeFilter) ensuring server-side re-fetch on filter change
LeadsList.js: Client-side filtering code has been removed, all filtering is server-side
server.py: get_leads endpoint correctly handles time_filter with proper ISO string date comparison
context_for_next_testing_agent: Server-side filtering for Leads List is fully functional. Network requests verified: time_filter, territory, assigned_to parameters are correctly passed to /api/leads endpoint. Backend returns filtered counts: this_week=2, this_month=53, lifetime=66 leads.
